skill.name: product-strategist
skill.description: Strategic product leadership toolkit for OKR cascades, market analysis, product vision, metrics, and product team design.
skill.summary: Create aligned product strategy, OKRs, metrics, and team plans for product leadership work.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and shell execution patterns, but review found these are false positives. The weak-crypto matches are ordinary product strategy text and dictionary values, while the backtick usage is Markdown documentation for running the local Python script.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: Static findings at SKILL.md lines 3, 14, and 20 and scripts/okr_cascade_generator.py line 368 do not show cryptographic code. They are plain product strategy descriptions and template text, with no hashing, encryption, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain descriptive words and OKR translation text, not crypto APIs or weak algorithms. There is no security-sensitive operation at these lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive Shell Execution Match
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 22 contains a Markdown usage example for running python scripts/okr_cascade_generator.py. It does not execute a command from the skill file and does not interpolate untrusted input into a shell call.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a documentation line enclosed in Markdown backticks. The reviewed Python script uses command-line arguments for strategy selection and printing output, not shell execution.

low_findings.title: Local Python Script Requires Normal Review
low_findings.description: The skill includes scripts/okr_cascade_generator.py, a local OKR generation script. Review found no network calls, file writes, secret access, or subprocess execution, so the residual concern is limited to normal community script review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script is present and executable as a local helper. Its observed behavior is deterministic OKR generation and console output, but community scripts should still be reviewed before use.

content.user_title: Align Product Strategy With OKR Cascades
content.value_statement: Product leaders often struggle to connect company goals with team execution. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code generate structured OKRs, strategy notes, metrics, and team alignment plans.
actual_capabilities: Generates company, product, and team OKR cascades from a selected strategy.
Creates alignment scores and contribution views for product objectives.
Supports market, competitive, vision, metrics, and organizational planning prompts.
Provides a Python helper script for OKR cascade generation and dashboard output.
Maps broad business goals into product and team-level objectives.
limitations: Uses template-based examples and does not validate strategy against live market data.
Does not integrate with product analytics, roadmap tools, or OKR platforms by default.
Requires human review before using generated OKRs for executive planning.
The script uses sample metrics unless the user adapts the inputs.

use_cases.title: Quarterly OKR Planning
use_cases.description: Create a first draft of company, product, and team OKRs for a new planning cycle.

use_cases.title: Strategy Alignment Review
use_cases.description: Translate company priorities into product initiatives and identify weak alignment across teams.

use_cases.title: Product Organization Design
use_cases.description: Plan team focus areas, ownership, and metrics for a scaling product organization.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Product OKRs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the product-strategist skill to create product OKRs for a growth strategy. Include three objectives, key results, owners, and simple success measures.

prompt_templates.title: Cascade Company Goals
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the product-strategist skill to cascade these company goals into product and team OKRs: [paste goals]. Show how each team contributes.

prompt_templates.title: Review Market Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the product-strategist skill to assess our market position, competitors, product vision, and KPI gaps for this business context: [paste context].

prompt_templates.title: Build an Executive Product Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the product-strategist skill to build an executive product strategy plan. Include vision, market assumptions, OKR cascade, team design, metrics, risks, and decision points.

output_examples.input: Growth strategy for a SaaS product entering a new segment.
output_examples.output: A structured OKR cascade with company acquisition goals, product adoption objectives, team-level key results, and an alignment score summary.

output_examples.input: Retention concerns after onboarding completion drops.
output_examples.output: A product strategy brief with retention objectives, customer value metrics, ownership recommendations, and follow-up research questions.

output_examples.input: Scaling from one product squad to four specialized teams.
output_examples.output: A team design proposal with focus areas, suggested owners, shared objectives, and KPI coverage checks.

best_practices: Provide current business goals, baseline metrics, target metrics, and planning horizon.
Ask for assumptions and risks so leaders can review the strategy before adoption.
Adapt generated OKRs with team capacity, customer evidence, and executive priorities.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated OKRs as final commitments without leadership review.
Do not use sample metrics when real baseline and target data are available.
Do not ask for competitive conclusions without providing market context or evidence.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps create product strategy, OKR cascades, market analysis prompts, metrics, and team alignment plans.

faq.question: Does it use live market data?
faq.answer: No. It works from provided context and templates unless the user supplies current research or data.

faq.question: Can it generate team-level OKRs?
faq.answer: Yes. The included script and prompts can cascade company and product goals into team-level objectives.

faq.question: Is it suitable for executive planning?
faq.answer: It can produce executive-ready drafts, but final plans should be reviewed by product and business leaders.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide company goals, product context, customer segments, baseline metrics, targets, team structure, and constraints.
